agentRules: Raskolnikov is a Rust project. Follow Rust idioms and the existing code style.
--- Naming ---
Structs: UpperCamelCase (Config, AgentShell, OllamaProvider)
Enums: UpperCamelCase (AppState, Role, ProviderKind)
Functions/Methods: snake_case (load, add_finding, parse_nmap_xml)
Variables: snake_case (tool_names, current_port)
Constants: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (BUILTIN_WORDLIST)
Unit structs (no data) used as namespaces: PromptBuilder, Transcript, FindingsExport
--- Module organisation ---
Top-level modules are declared in src/lib.rs as pub mod.
Each sub-module's mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations (no re-exports or pub use).
No barrel exports / prelude pattern. Reference via full path: crate::tools::nmap::NmapPort.
Flat within each module — no nesting beyond 2 levels.
Module structure must match docs/spec-mvp.md Project Structure section.
--- Code style ---
All struct fields are public — no getters/setters, direct field access throughout.
Constructors via named methods: .new(...) for stateful objects.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)] on data structs.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] on config structs with #[serde(default)].
Use #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)] on value-like enums and structs used in comparisons.
Imports: std first, then external crates, then crate-internal — grouped with blank lines.
No wildcard imports except use super::* in tests.
Internal modules use super:: to reference sibling types.
Prefer match over if-let chains. Prefer iterators over explicit loops where clearer.
--- Error handling ---
Use thiserror for error enums. Use crate-level pub type Result<T, E = Error> aliases.
Contextualise errors with map_err / context rather than raw ? propagation.
eprintln! for startup warnings only. Use tracing for runtime diagnostics.
Tests use .unwrap() or .expect() — no test-specific error handling.
--- Async patterns ---
Tokio with features = ["full"].
Async I/O via tokio::process, tokio::io, tokio::time, tokio::select!.
Use tokio::sync::watch::channel for interrupt signalling.
HTTP via reqwest — no SDKs.
Provider trait uses #[async_trait].
--- Testing ---
Inline tests: #[cfg(test)] mod tests { use super::*; } in every source file.
No separate tests/ directory. No test utility crate.
Async tests use #[tokio::test].
Mock HTTP with wiremock. Mock filesystem with tempfile.
Mock env vars with temp-env.
Tests must be deterministic — no real tools or AI providers.
Cover: happy path, error cases, edge cases (empty output, duplicates, timeouts).
--- Config ---
TOML-based with serde. All fields use #[serde(default)] with standalone fn default_*().
API keys from environment variables only — never in config files or logs.
Config merge order: built-in defaults → config.toml → env vars → CLI flags.
--- Documentation ---
docs/spec-mvp.md is the authoritative spec — keep it in sync with implementation.
Add Rustdoc /// comments on public API items (structs, traits, public functions).
Add // Comments on non-trivial internal logic (why, not what).
Conventional commits: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore.
Keep commits atomic — one logical change per commit.
No commit unless explicitly asked by the user.
--- Before submitting changes ---
Run: cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy && cargo fmt --check.
Fix all warnings. No dead code. No unwrap() in production code (use ? or expect with message).
--- Security ---
Sanitise shell arguments — never pass user input raw to shell commands.
Never log API keys, session data, or findings to stdout/stderr.
Session files get 0600 permissions. No telemetry. No phone-home.
